choices.summary: +1.0 effort · he tightens his own behavior · -1 sanity (he carries it)

choices.label: cross-check against the levee thread · ask the regulars what they saw.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · finds two more gauges than the official record · requires reading MS_00B_LEVEE

stages.title: the dampening · close the wake
stages.body:
The wake is the wake and now it has to close. Weir is on the dock at 8 AM · Moth is in the field at noon · the Mobile harbor-master is on the line at 1 PM asking if anyone wants to file what they saw.

Three ways to dampen it · each is its own cost.

choices.label: weir takes the dock · standard dampening · the harbor-master gets a polite no-comment.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the wake closes · weir is offline for one day · marker: weir_on_the_dock for 1 day

effects.log_line: [color=#c8a8ff]Marker set:[/color] Weir on the dock (1 day). Steamboat runs are on hold; the river is Weir's for the day.
effects.expiry_line: [color=#a8c0a8]Weir came off the dock at 5 AM. The river returned to its normal Sunday self.[/color]

choices.label: moth carries the dampening · the wake closes through the trees, not the river.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the wake closes in the wrong direction · -1 sanity for moth · slow burn

choices.label: let the harbor-master file it · own the paper trail · clean public response.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the wake closes officially · -1 cover · -1 future cover-recovery

effects.reason: the wake is on the harbor-master's paper trail

resolution_flavor_success: The river dropped four inches by Saturday morning. The harbor-master did not call back.
Weir stood on the dock until the wake closed. He did not speak to Steamboat about it.
resolution_flavor_failure: The wake did not close. By Sunday it had registered in three more counties. Steamboat called the agent at 11 PM and the agent did not pick up.

templates.title: Cathedral Basement Relay
templates.flavor: The W14 storm came north of the keys. The cathedral basement is the inland relay for the gulf-coast circle. JF is on the boiler; the line stays up only as long as someone's at the desk. Cross-BBS coordination through the storm window.

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.text: [color=#ff9090]The relay dropped at 11:48 PM. STEEPLE tried to call back from Mobile and got no carrier.[/color]

if_unresolved_at_severity_7.reason: the relay dropped; the gulf-coast circle holds it against you

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The storm window is the storm window. JF holds the boiler · someone holds the desk · someone holds the line up the coast. Three stages, one per role · this is the W14 hard branch and it stays in the file.

stages.title: the boiler · JF · the noise that holds the line
stages.body:
{agent} got to the cathedral basement at 7:14 PM Friday. The boiler is on its winter cycle even though it's August · JF said it has to be. The pilot is the pilot · the relief valve is the relief valve. JF is forty feet from the desk and he can hear the line by the way the boiler hums.

How the boiler is run during the storm decides what the line sounds like.

choices.label: JF runs the boiler · agent stays out of his way.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · JF carries it · costs JF 2 days of capacity post-storm

choices.label: agent works the boiler alongside JF · learns the rhythm.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the agent picks up the boiler skill for future storms · -1 sanity

choices.label: swap steamboat in · he knows the gulf-coast cycle · JF takes the desk.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · steamboat on boiler · cleanest under pressure · -1 cover (steamboat is now on the basement schedule)

effects.reason: steamboat on the cathedral basement schedule

stages.title: the desk · the line stays up
stages.body:
The desk is a folding table, a CB radio (modified for the relay frequency), a notebook, and three pens of different colors. Whoever's at the desk reads the carrier into the notebook every fifteen minutes. STEEPLE comes through at 9 PM, 11 PM, 1 AM, 3 AM, 5 AM. The desk stays manned until 6:14 AM Saturday.

Who holds the desk shapes what the gulf-coast circle remembers about this storm.

choices.label: agent holds the desk · all night · five carrier reads · no swap.
choices.summary: +2.0 effort · clean · -2 sanity · the cleanest log · marker: cathedral_relay_active for 3 days

effects.log_line: [color=#c8a8ff]Marker set:[/color] cathedral relay active (3 days). The basement is the gulf-coast circle's inland hub through the W14 storm window.
effects.expiry_line: [color=#a8c0a8]The cathedral relay stood down at 6:14 AM Saturday. STEEPLE logged the all-clear.[/color]

choices.label: two-shift rotation · agent + weir · weir takes 1 AM to 5 AM.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · clean enough · weir spent one night · agent stays functional Saturday

choices.label: tap the MS_010 storm-watch readers · ask one of the regulars to sit the desk.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · pulls a civilian into the relay · -2 cover · cleanest log next-day · requires the storm-watch thread

effects.reason: civilian pulled into the basement relay

stages.title: the coast · the call south
stages.body:
5:48 AM Saturday. The storm is north of the keys but still inland. STEEPLE is on the line from Mobile. The gulf-coast circle wants the all-clear or the hold. Whoever picks up the basement carrier carries the call south.

This is the last stage of the relay. The choice carries through W15 and beyond.

choices.label: all-clear · we held the relay · gulf circle continues normal.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · clean exit · the circle is intact

choices.label: hold · we held but the line is thin · gulf circle goes quiet for 72 hours.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · safer · costs 3 days of gulf-coast inbound traffic

choices.label: escalate · the relay is degraded · ask STEEPLE to route through Pensacola next storm.
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · the circle reshuffles · -1 cover (you're on the circle's notes now) · the cathedral isn't the relay next W14

effects.reason: agent named on the gulf-coast routing notes

resolution_flavor_success: The relay held. STEEPLE logged the carrier through at 5:48 AM. The boiler cooled by Sunday morning.
JF sat on the cathedral steps Saturday at 6:30 AM smoking a cigarette he hadn't smoked in three years. The line had stayed up.
resolution_flavor_failure: The relay dropped at 11:48 PM. STEEPLE tried to call back from Mobile and got no carrier. The gulf-coast circle holds it against you for the rest of the year.

templates.title: The Memorial Dock Plank
templates.flavor: The third plank from the shore end of the memorial dock has gone soft. The anniversary procession walks that dock in single file. Nobody has said the word 'liability' yet because saying it makes the procession a logistics problem, and it is not a logistics problem.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The plank went through under Mrs. Odile's cane at the vigil rehearsal. Nobody was hurt. Everybody was hurt.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Someone with hands and the sense not to make the repair a spectacle. The dock takes new wood fine; what it can't take is a CLOSED sign in anniversary week.

stages.title: under the dock, low tide
stages.body:
{agent} is under the dock at 6:40 AM with the tide out and the good plank stock from Verret's yard. The soft plank comes up in one piece — rot from the underside, five years of it. Two more planks are borderline.

The procession is in nine days. Verret's has enough stock for all three, but three planks means a morning of visible work on a dock people prefer to think of as permanent.

choices.label: replace all three. do it today, do it once.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the whole repair · visible work, honest work

choices.label: replace the soft one. flag the borderline pair for September.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · minimal footprint · a note in the drawer

choices.label: check the levee gauge thread first — the water table note.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · why the rot is climbing · requires MS_00B_LEVEE

effects.text: The gauge thread was right: the rot is climbing because the river is. The repair holds; the reason is filed.

resolution_flavor_success: The procession crossed in single file. Nobody looked down. That was the whole job: nobody looking down.
{agent} left the sawdust under the dock for the tide to take. By the anniversary the new wood had weathered half a shade. Close enough.
resolution_flavor_failure: The CLOSED sign went up in anniversary week. It was the correct sign and everyone hated it correctly.
The repair got done loudly. Now the dock is a thing people check with their foot, every crossing, forever.

templates.title: The Procession Permit
templates.flavor: The county's new events form asks the anniversary procession to declare an 'expected attendance' and an 'amplification plan.' The procession has happened every year since before the form. The form does not have a field for that.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The permit lapsed into a public agenda item. Now there is a MEETING about the procession, which is the one thing the procession never needed.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Paperwork with feelings in it. Send someone who can fill in a form so completely that no county employee ever has to think about the procession again.

stages.title: the county annex, window 4
stages.body: {agent} has the form, the clipboard, and the 2:00 PM slot at window 4. The clerk is new. The clerk is trying. The clerk asks what the procession is FOR, and means it kindly, and the room goes one degree colder anyway.

choices.label: answer the question the form is asking, not the one the clerk asked.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · attendance: 'all of us' becomes a number · clean file

choices.label: answer the clerk's question. take the extra twenty minutes.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the clerk learns what the procession is for · an ally at window 4

choices.label: file under the parish's standing exemption instead.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · faster · spends standing (the exemption gets noticed)

effects.reason: the standing exemption drew a supervisor's initials

resolution_flavor_success: The permit came back stamped. The procession will not know the form existed, which is the correct amount of knowing.
{agent} wrote 'no amplification · voices only' in the amplification-plan field. The county accepted it. It was true.
resolution_flavor_failure: The form bounced for a missing field. The missing field was invented last quarter. The procession is now 'pending.'

templates.title: The Retention Pond
templates.flavor: The retention pond behind the middle school has started smelling wrong — not the ordinary warm-algae August smell, which everyone has an accommodation with, but something underneath it. The kids noticed first. The kids always notice first.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The smell made the newsletter as 'seasonal pond conditions.' Three parents replied-all. The thread is now about property values.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Someone who can take a water sample without it becoming a scene. The outfall pipe matters more than the pond.

stages.title: the storm drain outfall, 7 AM
stages.body:
{agent} is at the outfall before the school traffic. The sample kit is in a lunch cooler because a lunch cooler is invisible. The water at the pipe mouth has a sheen the pond water doesn't.

Upstream, the pipe runs under the NexCorp lot's chain-link. That is a fact about plumbing. So far it is only a fact about plumbing.

choices.label: sample the outfall and the pond. keep the chain of custody clean.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · defensible numbers · slow lab week

choices.label: walk the pipe run up to the fence line. just walk it.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · eyes on the easement · someone may have eyes on you

effects.reason: a man at a fence line at 7 AM gets remembered

choices.label: fix the symptom: aerate the pond through the school's booster.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · the smell fades · the sheen does not

effects.reason: the pond looked fine for three weeks; the outfall kept its opinion

resolution_flavor_success: The smell went back to ordinary August. The sample results live in a folder that exists now, which is the real resolution.
The kids stopped holding their noses on the path. The kids are the gauge that matters.
resolution_flavor_failure: The pond cleared on its own, the way things clear on their own when someone upstream gets careful. The folder stayed empty.

templates.title: The Letters on Verbena Way
templates.flavor: Six houses on Verbena Way got the same letter in the same week: an 'unsolicited purchase interest' from an LLC with a P.O. box and a name like a golf course. Above-market offers, quick close, as-is. Mrs. Salazar brought hers to the pool and read it out loud, which is the only reason anyone knows it was six.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: Two of the six sold. The new owners have not moved in. The lawn service comes anyway, on the same day, in the same trucks.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Late-summer weight class. Send someone who reads county records for pleasure, or someone the six households will actually let in the door. Ideally both, in one dispatch, which is the problem.

stages.title: Mrs. Salazar's kitchen, Thursday
stages.body:
Four of the six households fit around Mrs. Salazar's kitchen table if the leaf goes in. The letters are in a stack by the fruit bowl. They are identical except for the addresses and one of them — the Kowalskis' — offers four percent more, and nobody wants to be the one to say so out loud.

{agent} has the county parcel map folded to the right page. The six houses make a shape on it.

choices.label: show the map. let the shape do the talking.
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the six see the easement corridor · the table decides together

choices.label: run the LLC's paper: registered agent, prior purchases, the pattern.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · names behind the P.O. box · takes the week

effects.text: The LLC's registered agent has registered eleven LLCs. All golf courses. None of them golf.

choices.label: advise the four percent question be said out loud, tonight.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the hard conversation now · the table survives it or it doesn't

effects.reason: the four percent conversation cost the room something

resolution_flavor_success: Nobody sold this month. The letters went in a shared folder, which is a thing the street has now: a shared folder, and the shape on the map.
The LLC's second round of letters came with a deadline. Deadlines are what you add when the first round didn't work. The street noticed that too.
resolution_flavor_failure: The Kowalskis took the four percent. No one blames them at the pool. Everyone blames them a little at the pool.
The letters stopped. The quiet after an offer withdrawn is not the same as the quiet before it arrived, and the street knows the difference now.

templates.title: The Pool Log
templates.flavor: The community pool's chemical log shows perfect numbers at 6 AM every day including the Tuesday the pump was broken, which means Dennis is filling it in from the porch. The water is fine. The log is fiction. Small fictions are how larger ones get their start in a town like this.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: A parent photographed the log next to the cloudy shallow end. The HOA now has a Pool Integrity Subcommittee. It has a lanyard.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: This is a Dennis problem wearing a chemistry problem's clothes. Send patience.

stages.title: the pump shed, 6:05 AM
stages.body: {agent} is at the pump shed when Dennis arrives at 6:05 with the log under his arm, already filled in. Dennis is seventy-one. Dennis has kept this pool since 1994. Dennis's knees do not do the pump-shed stairs anymore, and the test kit lives at the bottom of them.

choices.label: move the test kit to the top of the stairs. say nothing else.
choices.summary: +0.8 effort · the log becomes true again · Dennis keeps the pool

choices.label: offer to split the log: Dennis mornings, a kid evenings.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · succession, gently begun · Dennis hears what it is

choices.label: re-test everything for a week and backfill the real numbers.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · a true log · Dennis finds out someone checked

effects.reason: Dennis found the second set of handwriting in his log

resolution_flavor_success: The 6 AM numbers vary now, the way true numbers do. Nobody but {agent} knows why that's the good sign.
Dennis taught the evening kid the two-drop trick. The log has two hands in it and both of them are honest.
resolution_flavor_failure: The subcommittee bought a digital logger. It is accurate. Dennis waves from the porch and does not come down.

templates.title: Lights on the Ridge
templates.flavor: Two nights running, lights on the ridge east of the planting — not headlights, work lights, the kind you set on stands. Gone by dawn, no tracks on the road side. Somebody is surveying something at night, on the one ridge with sightline to the seed, and paying cash for the privilege of not being asked.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The lights stopped. The night after they stopped, the western contact missed a check-in for the first time in nine weeks.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Watching the watchers. Send someone who can spend two nights on a cold ridge and come back with facts instead of theories — or a demon, who will come back with both.

stages.title: the counter-watch, 11 PM
stages.body:
{agent} is prone in the treeline with the good glasses. The lights come on at 11:40: three stands, a total station on a tripod, two figures working a grid with the unhurried competence of people billing by the night. They are shooting elevations. They are shooting them TOWARD the planting.

A third figure sits in the truck the whole time. The third figure never gets out. That is the detail worth the cold.

choices.label: log plates, grid stakes, and the total station's make. touch nothing.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · a clean intelligence picture · they never know

choices.label: pull two of their grid stakes before dawn. cost them a night.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · buys a week · tells them someone walks this ridge

effects.reason: the re-survey crew came back with a fourth man who watched the treeline
pulled stakes are a message with a return address

choices.label: cross-check the elevations against the levee gauge history.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · WHY they want the ridge · requires MS_00B_LEVEE

effects.text: The elevations only make sense if you're planning to move water. Someone is planning to move water away from the planting.

resolution_flavor_success: The lights have not come back. The folder on the third figure is one page. One page is more than zero pages.
{agent} came off the ridge at 4 AM with numbers. Numbers keep. Theories don't.
resolution_flavor_failure: Third night: no lights. The survey finished early. Whatever they needed, they have it, and the planting is downhill of it.

templates.title: The Taste at Three Houses
templates.flavor: Three houses on the dirt-road side say the well water's taste changed — mineral, faintly sweet, 'like a coin held too long.' Same aquifer as the planting. The locals mention it sideways, at the store, the way they mention things they hope will be taken seriously without their having to insist.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The taste reached the fourth house. The fourth house is the planting's caretaker. What the wells drink, the ground drinks first.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The locals who walk the dirt road know which houses. Send someone they will talk to on a porch, not someone with a clipboard.

stages.title: three porches, one afternoon
stages.body: {agent} does the three porches in walking order. Same story, three tellings: the taste turned inside the same week, stronger in the morning, gone if you let the tap run long. The third porch belongs to Alma, who kept the old spring log before the county capped the spring, and Alma has been WAITING for someone to ask.

choices.label: sit with Alma's spring log. the whole afternoon.
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · forty years of the aquifer's moods · the pattern is in there

effects.text: Alma's log shows the coin taste twice before: 1961 and 1987. Both times, two seasons before the ground went strange.

choices.label: sample all three wells + the planting's irrigation head.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · numbers for the folder · the porches note the kit

choices.label: advise the run-the-tap workaround while the season turns.
choices.summary: +0.6 effort · the taste manageable · the question deferred

effects.reason: deferred aquifer questions come back with interest

resolution_flavor_success: The taste faded with the first cool week, the way Alma's log said it would. The log is photocopied now. Alma kept the original, correctly.
Three porches, one answer, zero clipboards. The dirt road talks to {agent} now.
resolution_flavor_failure: The fourth house tasted the coin. Nobody said the planting's name out loud. Everybody drank their coffee black that week.

templates.title: The Bleed on the Baby Monitors
templates.flavor: Something on the tower's band is bleeding into the valley's baby monitors between 2 and 4 AM — a warble, rhythmic, not voice, not quite not-voice. Four households have heard it. One of them has a cousin at the county paper. The clock on this one is the cousin.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: THE VALLEY'S STRANGE SIGNAL ran above the fold, with a phone number for 'others who have heard it.' The number has been busy for three days.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Half RF work, half porch work. The fix is a filter; the problem is four families who heard something in the nursery at 3 AM and deserve better than 'harmonics.'

stages.title: the fourth household, evening
stages.body: {agent} has the notch filters — cheap, clip-on, honest hardware that will end the bleed at all four houses tonight. The fourth household is the one with the cousin. The mother hands {agent} the monitor and plays the recording she made on the answering machine, and there in the warble, for half a second, is the shape of the thing nobody in this valley has agreed out loud to have heard.

choices.label: fit the filter. call it band harmonics. leave.
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the bleed ends · the recording remains

effects.reason: the recording made it to the cousin anyway

choices.label: fit the filter AND ask for the tape. trade her the truth she can carry: the tower's band is licensed, the license is real, the warble is the licensee's problem and it is being handled.
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · the tape retired · a household that feels answered

choices.label: take the recording to the caliche board before the cousin gets it.
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the valley's own forum hears it first · requires reading BP_014

effects.text: The board's old hands identified the warble in forty minutes and closed the thread with a filter diagram. The county paper cannot compete with that.

resolution_flavor_success: Four nurseries went quiet at 2 AM. The cousin's editor spiked the piece for lack of a second source. The second source is asleep.
The filter diagram is on four refrigerators now. The valley fixes its own strange, given the chance.
resolution_flavor_failure: The piece ran. It was kinder than feared and worse than nothing. The phrase 'the valley's strange signal' has a shelf life of years.

templates.title: The Charter Review
templates.flavor: The county has opened a 'routine decennial review' of the Graustark charter, eight years early. The review committee has three members, two of whom nobody has met, and its first request is the co-op lease registry going back to 1962. Routine things do not arrive certified mail.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The review's preliminary findings leak to the county paper before they are findings. That is what preliminary means now.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Paper weight-class. This wants someone who can read sixty years of leases faster than the committee can, and who the clerk's office will pull boxes for.

stages.title: The certified envelope
stages.body:
The lease registry request sits on the desk with its green certified-mail sticker. Sixty years of co-op paper, some of it in hands that are gone now. What the committee finds depends entirely on who assembles the boxes.

{agent} knows where the 1962 originals actually are, which is not where the index says.

choices.label: assemble the boxes yourselves, complete and in order, before they ask twice
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · nothing to find because nothing is hidden · the long clean way

effects.text: Complete records are the most boring weapon there is. The committee's second meeting is shorter than its first.

choices.label: answer exactly what was asked and not one page more
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · lawful minimum · the review stays hungry

effects.reason: the committee's second request is wider than the first · minimum compliance taught them where to dig

choices.label: find out who put the review on the county calendar eight years early
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · the question behind the question

effects.text: The calendar request came through a law office in the county seat. The law office's other client this year is an LLC named like a golf course.

resolution_flavor_success: The review closes with a two-line finding: records in good order. Sixty years of co-op paper goes back in the boxes, in order, where it has always been.
The committee's two unmet members never do get met. Their signatures close the file all the same.
resolution_flavor_failure: The review does not close. It 'remains open pending further materials,' which is county for whenever we want.
One lease from 1974 could not be produced. One is enough to keep a file open forever, and someone knew that when they asked.

templates.title: The Second Procession
templates.flavor: A procession is forming on the riverfront for Mr. Aldous Petric, who is not dead. He is eighty-one and in the county hospital and stable, and the town has started grieving him anyway, in advance, in the old way, with the candles and the slow walk. Pre-grief is still grief. It is also a rehearsal, and rehearsals become habits.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The procession walks a third night. Someone at the back has started carrying a candle for a name nobody recognizes.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Grief weight-class with a living subject · this wants the gentlest hands on the roster and someone who can talk to the hospital.

stages.title: Candles for a living man
stages.body:
Thirty walkers, river to cathedral, for a man who is at this moment eating hospital pudding and complaining about the television mount. The walk is beautiful. The walk is wrong. Both things are true and the town knows both, and walks anyway.

{agent} can reach the hospital by phone, or the procession by joining it.

choices.label: put Mr. Petric himself on the phone with the front of the procession
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the living voice interrupts the rehearsal

effects.text: 'You are all very kind and I am not done yet.' The procession laughs, wetly, and becomes a get-well line at the pharmacy card rack.

choices.label: join the walk and steer it · grief will have its walk, aim it somewhere true
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · redirect, don't refuse

effects.text: The walk ends at the memorial dock instead of the cathedral, at the names already carved. Old grief absorbs new rehearsal. The candles get used honestly.

choices.label: ask who started it · processions have first walkers
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · the organizer question

effects.text: The first walker was Mrs. Okafor, who buried a husband in March and has been early to everything since. The procession was never about Petric.

resolution_flavor_success: Mr. Petric comes home Thursday to a lawn full of get-well cards and one candle, which he keeps.
The procession does not walk again. The town remembers it can, which was maybe the point.
resolution_flavor_failure: The procession walks nightly now. Attendance is steady. Mr. Petric, home and well, has been asked twice not to attend his own.
The rehearsal became the habit. When the town needs the real thing, it will be very, very good at it.

templates.title: An Offer on the Model Home
templates.flavor: A couple from out of county wants to buy the model home. Cash, quick close, furniture included. The model home is not for sale because the model home is the idea of the neighborhood, furnished. Selling it would be selling the idea. The offer is, everyone admits, extremely good.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The couple's agent, declined, asks a different question on the way out: which OTHER houses on this street might entertain offers? He writes down the answer nobody gives him.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: This is Mackenzie's house in every sense · but the negotiation wants someone who can say no in a way that doesn't end the conversation.

stages.title: The couple, the kitchen, the offer
stages.body:
The couple stands in the model kitchen holding the offer folder like a casserole. They are not developers. They are just people who walked through the idea of the neighborhood and want to live inside it, which is either the best endorsement Harmony Creek ever got or the beginning of the end of the tour.

{agent} has the listing sheet that does not exist, because the house is not listed.

choices.label: decline warmly and walk them to the three phase-four lots instead
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · redirect the want to real inventory

effects.text: They pick the corner lot with the same floor plan. It will not have the furniture. Mackenzie sends them the paint codes, which is the furniture, really.

choices.label: name the real reason no · the model home is load-bearing
choices.summary: +1.1 effort · honesty about the idea

effects.text: 'It's the neighborhood's dream of itself. If someone lives in it, the street stops being able to see it.' The couple, to their eternal credit, gets it.

choices.label: float the number past the HOA quietly · everything has a price, know yours
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · a dangerous piece of information to create

effects.reason: the model home has a number now · numbers, once said, do not unsay

resolution_flavor_success: The model home stays the model home. The couple closes on the corner lot and hosts the first non-model open house in phase four, which Mackenzie attends, and cries at, briefly, in the good way.
The offer folder goes in the HOA archive, unopened. Some numbers are better as rumors.
resolution_flavor_failure: The couple withdrew, politely. The story of the offer did not withdraw. Every owner on the street now knows the model home has a market.
The furniture stays. The idea has a price tag on it now, tucked under the fruit bowl where everyone knows it is.

templates.title: The Out-of-Season Census
templates.flavor: A man with a lanyard and a clipboard is going door to door in Harmony Creek 'for the census.' The census is not this year. The lanyard is laminated well. His questions start normal and end with how long have your neighbors lived here and would you say the community is close-knit. He writes down the pauses.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The stranger stops working Harmony Creek. A week later a car with the same rental sticker is parked, mornings, on the Small Wood ridge road.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Counter-surveillance weight-class · someone who can be walked door to door BEHIND him, or someone fast enough to make his afternoon very unproductive.

stages.title: Four doors ahead of him
stages.body:
He is four doors into Verbena Way at a steady eleven minutes a door. The houses he has finished all report the same feeling: perfectly polite, wrote more than we said.

{agent} can meet him at the fifth door, or work the street ahead of him, or follow the rental car at five.

choices.label: brief the street ahead of him · every door answers with the same three sentences
choices.summary: +1.7 effort · the neighborhood becomes a wall of pleasant nothing

effects.text: Eleven doors, one voice. By the ninth identical answer he is writing nothing. A wall of pleasant nothing is still a wall.

choices.label: meet him at the fifth door with county ID requirements and a smile
choices.summary: +1.3 effort · paperwork as fence

effects.text: He produces a supervisor's number that rings to a voicemail box that is full. 'They're bad about that,' he says, already leaving.

choices.label: follow the rental at end of day · where a clipboard sleeps says who holds it
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · the tail

effects.text: The rental returns to a county-seat lot that also holds, at this hour, one car from a law office you have read about lately.

resolution_flavor_success: The clipboard stops coming. The pauses he collected are eleven doors of the same pleasant nothing, which is what a close-knit community actually sounds like from outside.
The lanyard is in a drawer somewhere with the others. The street keeps the three sentences, just in case.
resolution_flavor_failure: He finished the street. Somewhere there is now a folder in which Harmony Creek is a set of pauses, measured.
The census that wasn't ended on its own schedule, which is the worst way for it to end · it means the schedule was met.

templates.title: Three Dark Days and One Lit Window
templates.flavor: The storm took Small Wood's power for three days. Generators, coolers, the fire hall running a phone charging table · the town handled it the way the town handles things. What the town cannot handle is that for all three dark days, the tower's window stayed lit. An emergency meeting has been called, and the agenda has one item, and the item is a question.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The meeting ends without an answer. Two attendees do not go home afterward · they walk, instead, up the ridge road, together, quietly.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The hardest room in the game · a town that deserves an answer, an answer that cannot be given, and a meeting that must end anyway. Send whoever you trust most in front of people.

stages.title: The fire hall, folding chairs, every seat taken
stages.body:
Forty-one residents, the fire captain moderating, and the question on the whiteboard in dry-erase: WHAT POWERS THE TOWER. Nobody is angry yet. Small Wood does not do angry first · it does quiet, then organized, then gone.

{agent} has a seat at the front and every eye in the room.

choices.label: answer with everything true that can be said · the grid maps, the easement, the honest unknown
choices.summary: +1.8 effort · respect as strategy · the unknown named out loud

effects.text: 'We don't know, and here is everything we do.' Small Wood can live with an honest unknown. It has been living next to one for years.

choices.label: redirect to the grid postmortem · give the room a fixable thing
choices.summary: +1.3 effort · the answerable question swap

effects.reason: the postmortem is due · the room remembers what it was promised instead of an answer

choices.label: let the fire captain run long · rooms answer themselves if you give them time
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · patience play · risky with this town

effects.text: Hour three. The question on the whiteboard has grown two sub-questions and lost none. Small Wood is very good at meetings, which is the problem.

resolution_flavor_success: The meeting adjourns at a reasonable hour, which in Small Wood is the strongest possible outcome. The whiteboard is erased by the fire captain personally.
The charging table becomes a monthly thing at the fire hall, no outage required. The town keeps the part of the dark days that was good.
resolution_flavor_failure: The meeting spawns a committee. The committee's first act is a records request. Its second is a walk up the ridge road with a camera.
Nobody says the word tower at the pharmacy anymore. They say 'the item.' A town with a code word is a town half-organized.

templates.title: The Replant Comes Up Facing Wrong
templates.flavor: The community garden's fall replant came up. All of it. Strong, green, healthy · and every stem, every row, every volunteer sunflower along the fence line, leaning the same direction. Sunflowers follow the sun. These are not following the sun. Mrs. Han measured the angle with her son's protractor and it points, she says, exactly where you think it points.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The garden committee replants the border rows facing 'correctly,' by hand, with stakes. By Sunday the stakes lean too.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Botanical weight-class with a dread floor · this wants someone the garden committee trusts AND someone who can take a measurement without making it a headline.

stages.title: Mrs. Han's measurement
stages.body:
The protractor sits on the potting table, angle marked in pencil. Twelve gardeners stand in a rough line looking at their own rows the way you look at a friend who has started talking differently.

{agent} can measure, or dig, or ask the older gardeners what the plot did in other strange years.

choices.label: measure everything, publish the numbers on the garden shed door
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · data in daylight beats rumor in the dark

effects.text: The angles are identical to the second decimal, which is worse and better · worse because it's real, better because real things get dealt with.

choices.label: ask the older gardeners about 1976's plot
choices.summary: +1.3 effort · the long memory

effects.text: Mr. Whitlock, eighty-nine, does not look up from his beans: 'It leaned in '76 too. Leaned back the next spring. Plants are polite. They're just saying hello.'

choices.label: quietly rotate the harvest schedule so the lean feeds people anyway
choices.summary: +1.1 effort · the practical answer · dinner over dread

effects.text: The tomatoes are excellent this year. Facing whatever they face, they are excellent, and twelve households eat them, facing forward.

resolution_flavor_success: The garden keeps its lean and its yield. The shed door numbers stay posted, fading honestly in the sun the plants ignore.
Mrs. Han keeps the protractor in the shed now, on a nail, like a tool. In Small Wood, measurement is a tool.
resolution_flavor_failure: Three plots went unharvested · their gardeners 'didn't feel right about eating it.' The food bank felt the shortfall by October.
Someone drove up from the county extension office, measured, said 'huh,' and left. The 'huh' is on the record now.

templates.title: The Records Request
templates.flavor: Fairway Meadows LLC has filed forty-one instruments with the county in six years and the index cards for eleven of them are 'temporarily unavailable.' A public records request is a polite way of saying: produce the cards or explain, on paper, why you can't. It is the first shot that is entirely legal to fire.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The request lapsed unanswered past its own deadline, which taught the clerk's office exactly how hard anyone was willing to push. The eleven cards are 'temporarily unavailable' with more confidence now.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Someone patient enough to sit in the reading room until closing, and pleasant enough that the clerk starts pulling boxes before being asked. Insight work. A demon in a records room reads fast but the certified stamps sit on them like wet wool.

stages.title: the reading room, second week
stages.body: {agent} has the eleven index numbers on a card and the card face-down on the table, which is how you ask a courthouse a question without the courthouse hearing you ask it. The clerk — Doris, thirty-one years at that counter — has begun to say 'you again' in the tone that is two visits from friendly.

choices.label: keep coming back daily · let Doris arrive at the boxes herself
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the slow lawful way · Doris becomes a fact on our side

effects.text: On the ninth day Doris pulls the eleventh box unasked and says, not looking up: 'funny how these all moved shelves the same week.' Funny, agrees {agent}, and writes down the week.

choices.label: file the formal demand today · statutory clock, certified copy
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · fast and loud · the law office learns we know the statute

effects.text: The demand lands with its green sticker. Within the week a junior associate is in the reading room pulling OUR filings. The paper war is now mutual, which was always going to happen, just not always going to happen this early.

templates.title: The Filing Deadline
templates.flavor: Their motion arrived Thursday at 4:58 PM, timestamped two minutes before the clerk's window closed, which is not an accident, it is a signature. The response window is fourteen days and the first three were the weekend and the holiday. This problem is a clock wearing a manila folder.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The deadline passed. The motion stands unopposed, and 'unopposed' is a word that gets read aloud in every hearing after.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Speed and stamina. The response wants drafting, noticing, serving, and filing, in that order, with no day off. This is the one problem on the board that genuinely cannot wait for the right person — it can only wait for a fast one.

stages.title: day ten of fourteen
stages.body: The draft response is nineteen pages and eleven of them are good. {agent} has found the weakness in their motion — a case they cited that was reversed in the spring and either they don't know or they're betting we don't.

choices.label: lead with the reversed case · embarrass the citation
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · wins the motion · the law office stops underestimating us

effects.text: Footnote four does the work. At the hearing their associate asks for a continuance in the voice of a man reading his own performance review.

choices.label: file the adequate eleven pages · save the reversal for a bigger motion
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · survives the clock · keeps powder dry

effects.text: Adequate, timely, unremarkable — the response holds the line and shows them nothing. Somewhere in a folder the reversed case waits like a coin under the floorboard.

templates.title: The Countersuit
templates.flavor: Fairway Meadows LLC v. the Graustark Community Cooperative, tortious interference, damages 'to be proven at trial.' It names the co-op, two officers, and — in a paragraph that has been read aloud at three kitchen tables — 'unknown associates John and Jane Doe 1 through 12.' Twelve Does. Somebody counted the summer's fingerprints and got twelve.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: Undefended, the countersuit's discovery demands reach back up the river: the LLC moves to reopen the charter review with the county, and this time they draft the scope.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: The heaviest paper on the board. Wants the co-op's best reader for weeks, not days — and wants somebody ELSE holding their normal work while they're gone. Losing this doesn't end the summer. It ends the co-op's next ten years.

stages.title: who counts as a Doe
stages.body: Their discovery request wants the co-op's correspondence 'with all agents, contractors, and associates' for the summer. {agent} reads it twice. Answered honestly and completely, the list of associates includes names that do not photograph well, and one that does not photograph at all.

choices.label: fight the discovery scope · protective order, narrow it to the parcels
choices.summary: +2.0 effort · the associates stay out of the record

effects.text: The judge narrows discovery to 'communications concerning the parcels at issue,' which is lawyer for: the co-op's friends are none of your business. In the gallery, somebody's shoulders come down an inch.

choices.label: answer broadly and bury them · forty boxes of everything
choices.summary: +1.5 effort · malicious compliance · risky for the quiet friends

effects.text: Forty boxes, indexed badly on purpose, delivered to their lobby at 4:58 PM on a Thursday. Petty? Certified petty. But somewhere in box thirty-one is a name that should not be in any box.

templates.title: The Public Comment Period
templates.flavor: The rezoning application — THEIR rezoning application, the one the golf course needs — has entered its thirty-day public comment period. Comments become part of the record. The record is what the appeal reads. This is the one arena where a town that shows up beats a company that files.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: Four comments were received: three from the LLC's consultants and one from a man concerned about geese. The record now shows a community that did not object.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: An organizer's problem, not a lawyer's. Wants someone the town already listens to, riding the phone tree — and wants the comments SPECIFIC. 'I oppose this' is one line in the record. 'The drainage study ignores the 1977 flood line' is a finding.

stages.title: the night of the hearing
stages.body: Forty-one people signed the speaking list and the fire marshal is counting the room. {agent} has the drainage numbers on cards, distributed — nine speakers, one point each, no repetition. The LLC's consultant has a laser pointer and the expression of a man who prepared for six geese and got a town.

choices.label: run the nine-speaker relay as drilled
choices.summary: +1.4 effort · nine findings enter the record in order

effects.text: Nine speakers, nine findings, no repetition. The chair — who has chaired four hundred of these — starts taking notes at speaker three, which the regulars say he has not done since the bypass.

choices.label: let the town speak in its own order and its own voice
choices.summary: +1.0 effort · messier record · the anger gets its night

effects.text: It runs long and hot and twice the chair reaches for the gavel and puts it down. Less usable on appeal — but forty-one people walked out of that room a town, and towns are load-bearing.

templates.title: The Registry Pull
templates.flavor: Who owns Fairway Meadows LLC. Four words, one registry, and the answer is a matryoshka: the LLC is managed by a second LLC registered to a third, and the third's registered agent is a mail drop in a strip mall two counties over. Somebody built this to be boring to open. Open it anyway.

if_unresolved_at_severity_6.text: The trail went cold at the mail drop and stayed there. Whoever built the matryoshka gets to keep being nobody, and nobody is very hard to sue.

templates.dispatch_shape_hint: Insight work, pure. Wants the co-op's best reader of boring paper — the kind of mind that notices two different documents were typed on the same machine. The trail is legal to follow every step; the last step is the one that wants nerve.

stages.title: the bottom of the matryoshka
stages.body: {agent} has walked the chain: three LLCs, two states, one mail drop. At the bottom, the original formation filing from 1981 — typed, pre-computer, the true name line filled in and then AMENDED, the amendment stapled over it. The staple is old. Nobody has lifted it in forty years. Lifting it is legal. It is also the kind of thing you can't unlift.

choices.label: lift the staple · read the original name
choices.summary: +1.6 effort · the answer, and everything the answer costs

effects.text: Under the amendment, the 1981 true-name line. Not a person. A seal — a county seal, properly embossed, from a county that is not on any map {agent} has ever read. {agent} photographs it, restaples it, and files the photograph under the floorboard of the mind where the summer keeps its worst facts.

choices.label: leave the staple · document the chain and stop at the drop
choices.summary: +1.2 effort · enough to fight with · the bottom stays closed

effects.text: The chain of shells, documented and notarized, is enough to pierce for litigation purposes. What's under the last staple stays under it. Some registries are load-bearing too.
